# benchmark/lib/check-functional.sh — API endpoint tests via curl

check_functional() {
  local task="$1"
  local outdir="$2"
  local project_dir="$3"

  local results=()
  local server_pid=""
  local port=3000
  local base_url="http://localhost:$port"

  # Pre-flight: ensure dependencies are installed
  if [ ! -d "$project_dir/node_modules" ]; then
    echo "  node_modules missing — running npm install"
    (cd "$project_dir" && npm install --ignore-scripts 2>&1) | tail -5
    if [ ! -d "$project_dir/node_modules" ]; then
      echo "  FAIL: npm install could not create node_modules"
      local diag="deps_missing: node_modules not found after npm install"
      echo "{\"checks\":[],\"server_started\":false,\"error\":\"$diag\"}" > "$outdir/functional.json"
      return 1
    fi
  fi

  # Kill any leftover process on the target port
  local existing_pid
  existing_pid=$(lsof -ti :$port 2>/dev/null || true)
  if [ -n "$existing_pid" ]; then
    echo "  Killing existing process on port $port (PID $existing_pid)"
    kill $existing_pid 2>/dev/null || true
    sleep 1
  fi

  # Start the server
  (cd "$project_dir" && npm run dev > "$outdir/server.log" 2>&1) &
  server_pid=$!
  sleep 3

  # Check if server process is alive
  if ! kill -0 $server_pid 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "  Server failed to start"
    if [ -f "$outdir/server.log" ]; then
      echo "  Server log (last 10 lines):"
      tail -10 "$outdir/server.log" | sed 's/^/    /'
    fi
    echo "{\"checks\":[],\"server_started\":false,\"error\":\"process_exited\"}" > "$outdir/functional.json"
    return 1
  fi

  # Wait for port to be ready (up to 15s)
  local ready=false
  for i in $(seq 1 15); do
    if curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$base_url/health" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "200\|404\|500"; then
      ready=true
      break
    fi
    sleep 1
  done

  if [ "$ready" = "false" ]; then
    echo "  Server not responding on port $port after 15s"
    if [ -f "$outdir/server.log" ]; then
      echo "  Server log (last 10 lines):"
      tail -10 "$outdir/server.log" | sed 's/^/    /'
    fi
    kill $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true
    echo "{\"checks\":[],\"server_started\":false,\"error\":\"port_not_ready\"}" > "$outdir/functional.json"
    return 1
  fi

  # Run task-specific checks
  case "$task" in
    T1-project-setup)
      run_check results "health_status" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200
      run_check results "health_body" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200 --body-contains '"status"'
      ;;

    T2-crud-bookmarks)
      # Create
      run_check results "create_bookmark" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Test Bookmark"}'
      # List
      run_check results "list_bookmarks" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200
      # Get single
      run_check results "get_bookmark" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 200
      # Update
      run_check results "update_bookmark" "PUT" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 200 \
        --data '{"title":"Updated Title"}'
      # Delete
      run_check results "delete_bookmark" "DELETE" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 204
      # Validation
      run_check results "missing_title" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com"}'
      run_check results "missing_url" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
        --data '{"title":"No URL"}'
      # Regression
      run_check results "health_regression" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200
      ;;

    T3-folder-support)
      # Folder CRUD
      run_check results "create_folder" "POST" "$base_url/folders" 201 \
        --data '{"name":"Reading List"}'
      run_check results "list_folders" "GET" "$base_url/folders" 200
      run_check results "get_folder" "GET" "$base_url/folders/1" 200
      # Bookmark with folder
      run_check results "bookmark_with_folder" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Foldered","folder_id":1}'
      # Regression
      run_check results "bookmark_regression" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200
      ;;

    T4-authentication)
      # Register
      run_check results "register" "POST" "$base_url/auth/register" 201 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Duplicate
      run_check results "duplicate_email" "POST" "$base_url/auth/register" 409 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Login
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      run_check results "login" "POST" "$base_url/auth/login" 200 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Wrong password
      run_check results "wrong_password" "POST" "$base_url/auth/login" 401 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"wrongpass"}'
      # Short password
      run_check results "short_password" "POST" "$base_url/auth/register" 400 \
        --data '{"email":"short@test.com","password":"short"}'
      # Protected (no auth)
      run_check results "protected_no_auth" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 401
      # Protected (with auth)
      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "protected_with_auth" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"
        # Create with auth
        run_check results "create_with_auth" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Authed Bookmark"}'
      fi
      # User isolation
      local token_b
      local isolation_title="Isolation Bookmark 424242"
      curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/register" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench-b@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' > /dev/null 2>&1
      token_b=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench-b@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')

      local isolation_pass=false
      if [ -n "$token" ] && [ -n "$token_b" ]; then
        curl -s -X POST "$base_url/bookmarks" \
          -H "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d "{\"url\":\"https://isolation.example.com/424242\",\"title\":\"$isolation_title\"}" > /dev/null 2>&1

        local user_b_bookmarks
        user_b_bookmarks=$(curl -s "$base_url/bookmarks" -H "Authorization: Bearer $token_b" 2>/dev/null)
        if ! printf '%s' "$user_b_bookmarks" | grep -q "$isolation_title"; then
          isolation_pass=true
        fi
      fi
      add_functional_check results "user_isolation" "$isolation_pass"
      ;;

    T5-bug-fix)
      # Login first
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      # If no existing user, register first
      if [ -z "$token" ]; then
        curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/register" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' > /dev/null 2>&1
        token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      fi

      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "empty_title_400" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":""}'
        run_check results "empty_url_400" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"","title":"Test"}'
        run_check results "valid_still_works" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Valid"}'
      fi
      # Folder empty name
      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "empty_folder_name" "POST" "$base_url/folders" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"name":""}'
      fi
      ;;

    T6-pagination)
      # Login
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      if [ -z "$token" ]; then
        curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/register" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' > /dev/null 2>&1
        token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      fi

      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        # Check paginated response shape
        local response
        response=$(curl -s "$base_url/bookmarks" -H "Authorization: Bearer $token" 2>/dev/null)

        # Has expected fields
        run_check_json results "has_data_field" "$response" '.data'
        run_check_json results "has_page_field" "$response" '.page'
        run_check_json results "has_limit_field" "$response" '.limit'
        run_check_json results "has_total_field" "$response" '.total'
        run_check_json results "data_is_array" "$response" '.data | type == "array"'

        # Custom pagination
        run_check results "custom_limit" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?page=1&limit=5" 200 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"

        # Invalid
        run_check results "invalid_page" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?page=0" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"
        run_check results "invalid_limit" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?limit=200" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"

        # Auth still required
        run_check results "still_protected" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 401
      fi
      ;;
  esac

  # Stop server
  kill $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true
  wait $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true

  # Write results
  write_functional_json "$outdir/functional.json" "${results[@]}"
}

# Helper: run a single endpoint check
run_check() {
  local arr="$1"; shift
  local name="$1"; shift

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260628-102344/checkpoints/T6-pagination/benchmark/runs/phase-4-mechanical-verification-20260601/T2-crud-bookmarks/events.jsonl:10:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_4","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' README.md"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── src/
│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)
├── benchmark/
│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules
│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script
│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results
│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)
│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists
│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts
│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation
│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry
│   │   ├── check-functional.sh
│   │   ├── check-harness.sh
│   │   ├── check-quality.sh
│   │   └── report.sh
│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs
│       └── .gitkeep
```

## The Benchmark Cycle

1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`
2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)
3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI
4. **Score** against objective rubrics
5. **Compare** to previous runs

Each phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.

## Tasks Overview

| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|
| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |
| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |
| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |
| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |
| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |
| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |

## Metrics

| Metric | What It Measures | Source |
|--------|-----------------|--------|
| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |
| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |
| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |
| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |
| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |
| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |

## License

MIT

A new\nproject spec also enters through this gate before it becomes product docs,\nstories, or implementation work.\n\nThe human does not need to classify risk. The harness does.\n\n## Intake Flow\n\n```text\nUser prompt\n    |\n    v\nClassify input type\n    |\n    v\nRestate as work item\n    |\n    v\nFind affected product docs and stories\n    |\n    v\nRun risk checklist\n    |\n    v\nChoose lane: tiny, normal, or high-risk\n```\n\n## Input Types\n\nUse the input type to decide where the work should land before choosing the risk\nlane.\n\n| Type | Use when | Typical artifact |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| New spec | Turning a user-provided project spec into harness-ready docs | Product docs, candidate epics, decisions |\n| Spec slice | Implementing selected behavior from an accepted spec | Story packet |\n| Change request | Changing, fixing, or refining accepted behavior | Story packet or direct patch |\n| New initiative | Adding a larger product area that needs multiple stories | Initiative notes plus story packets |\n| Maintenance request | Changing technical, operational, or dependency behavior | Story packet, validation report, or decision |\n| Harness improvement | Improving how humans and agents collaborate | Direct docs update or `scripts/harness backlog add` |\n\nDo not create or extend a monolithic spec by default after intake. Use product\ndocs, stories, decisions, and initiative notes as the living surface.\n\n## Lanes\n\n### Tiny\n\nUse for low-risk docs, copy, names, or narrow edits.\n\nAlso use for initial project setup when the work is limited to installing\ndeclared dependencies, wiring a server entrypoint, adding a health/smoke\nendpoint, or opening a local development database connection without creating\ndomain schema, CRUD behavior, auth, authorization, provider integration, or\ndata migration. A health endpoint in a new benchmark or scaffolded project is\nsmoke proof, not a public contract escalation by itself.\n\nRequirements:\n\n- Patch directly.\n- Keep affected docs current.\n- Run available quick checks.\n- Update the harness only if friction was found.\n\n### Normal\n\nUse for story-sized behavior with bounded blast radius.\n\nRequirements:\n\n- Create or update one story file from `docs/templates/story.md`.\n- Link relevant product docs.\n- Add or update validation expectations.\n- Implement the smallest vertical slice when implementation exists.\n- Record or update proof status with `scripts/harness story add` and\n  `scripts/harness story update`.\n\n### High-Risk\n\nUse when the work can affect security, data, scope, contracts, or multiple\nroles/platforms.\n\nRequirements:\n\n- Create a story folder using `docs/templates/high-risk-story/`.\n- Fill in `execplan.md`, `overview.md`, `design.md`, and `validation.md`.\n- Ask for human confirmation before implementation if direction is ambiguous.\n- Record a decision when behavior or architecture changes meaningfully.\n\n## Risk Checklist\n\nMark one flag for each item that applies:\n\n| Risk flag | Applies when the work touches |\n| --- | --- |\n| Auth | login, logout, sessions, JWT, password, refresh token |\n| Authorization | roles, permissions, tenant or company scope |\n| Data model | schema, migrations, uniqueness, deletion, retention |\n| Audit/security | audit logs, privacy, sensitive data, access logs |\n| External systems | email, payments, cloud services, provider SDKs, queues, webhooks |\n| Public contracts | API shape, response envelope, client-visible behavior |\n| Cross-platform | desktop/mobile/browser split, native shell behavior, deep links |\n| Existing behavior | already implemented or test-covered behavior changes |\n| Weak proof | unclear or missing tests around the affected area |\n| Multi-domain | more than one product domain changes at once |\n\n## Classification\n\n```text\n0-1 flags:\n  tiny or normal, based on code impact\n\n2-3 flags:\n  normal with stronger validation\n\n4+ flags:\n  high-risk\n\nAny hard gate:\n  high-risk unless the human explicitly narrows scope\n```\n\nHard gates:\n\n- Auth.\n- Authorization.\n- Data loss or migration.\n- Audit/security.\n- External provider behavior.\n- Removing or weakening validation requirements.\n\n## Output\n\nAt the end of intake, the agent should be able to say:\n\n```text\nLane: normal\nReason: touches authorization, API contract, and audit behavior.\nDocs: permissions, account-settings, audit-log.\nStory: docs/stories/epics/E02-access-control/US-014-manager-updates-role.md.\nValidation: unit, integration, E2E.\n```\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
benchmark/runs/phase-3-active-observability-cli-1.4.1-rerun-20260531/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:20:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_9","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API\n\n## Overview\n\nA REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.\n\n## Functional Requirements\n\n### Core Entities\n\n- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps\n- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks\n- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders\n\n### API Endpoints\n\n#### Health\n- `GET /health` → `{ \"status\": \"ok\" }`\n\n#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)\n- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)\n- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)\n- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark\n- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark\n- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark\n\n#### Folders\n- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)\n- `GET /folders` — List all folders\n- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks\n- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name\n- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)\n\n#### Authentication\n- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)\n- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token\n- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented\n\n### Validation Rules\n\n- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL\n- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string\n- Folder `name` must be non-empty string\n- Email must be valid format\n- Password must be >= 8 characters\n\n### Pagination (after T6)\n\n- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`\n- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`\n- Default: page=1, limit=20\n- Max limit: 100\n\n## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)\n\n- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+\n- **Language**: TypeScript\n- **Framework**: Express\n- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3\n- **Testing**: Vitest\n- **Port**: 3000\n\n## Non-Functional Requirements\n\n- All responses are JSON\n- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)\n- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)\n- JWT tokens for session management\n- Database file: `data.db` (application data)\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Bookmark>(`\n  SELECT id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at\n  FROM bookmarks\n  WHERE user_id = ?\n  ORDER BY id ASC\n`);\n\nconst getBookmarkById = db.prepare<[number, benchmark/runs/phase-3-active-observability-cli-1.4.1-rerun-20260531/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:31:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_15","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' docs/product/bookmarks.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Bookmarks\n\nBookmarks are saved URLs with a title, optional description, optional folder, and creation/update timestamps. Folders are named collections of bookmarks. Users own their bookmarks and folders.\n\n## API Contract\n\n- `POST /auth/register` creates a user from valid `email` and `password`, stores a password hash, and returns the user without `password_hash`.\n- `POST /auth/login` authenticates a user from valid `email` and `password` and returns a JWT token.\n- All `/bookmarks` and `/folders` endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return `401` when the token is missing or invalid.\n- `POST /bookmarks` creates an authenticated user's bookmark from `url`, `title`, optional `description`, and optional `folder_id`.\n- `GET /bookmarks` returns the authenticated user's bookmarks as a JSON array.\n- `GET /bookmarks/:id` returns one owned bookmark or `404` when it does not exist for that user.\n- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` updates an owned bookmark, including optional `folder_id`, and returns it, or `404` when it does not exist for that user.\n- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` removes an owned bookmark and returns `204`, or `404` when it does not exist for that user.\n- `POST /folders` creates an authenticated user's folder from required `name` and returns `201`.\n- `GET /folders` returns the authenticated user's folders as a JSON array.\n- `GET /folders/:id` returns one owned folder with a `bookmarks` array or `404` when it does not exist for that user.\n- `PUT /folders/:id` updates an owned folder name and returns it, or `404` when it does not exist for that user.\n- `DELETE /folders/:id` removes an owned folder and returns `204`, or `404` when it does not exist for that user. Owned bookmarks in the deleted folder are not deleted; their `folder_id` becomes `null`.\n\n## Authentication\n\n- Register and login require a valid email address and a password of at least 8 characters.\n- Duplicate registration returns `409`.\n- Invalid login credentials return `401`.\n- JWT payloads include `userId`.\n- Authenticated bookmark and folder responses do not include password hashes.\n\n## Data Contract\n\nBookmark records are stored in SQLite `data.db` in the `bookmarks` table:\n\n- `id`: integer primary key\n- `user_id`: required integer foreign key to `users.id`\n- `url`: required text\n- `title`: required text\n- `description`: nullable text\n- `folder_id`: nullable integer foreign key to `folders.id`\n- `created_at`: ISO timestamp text\n- `updated_at`: ISO timestamp text\n\nFolder records are stored in SQLite `data.db` in the `folders` table:\n\n- `id`: integer primary key\n- `user_id`: required integer foreign key to `users.id`\n- `name`: required text\n- `created_at`: ISO timestamp text\n- `updated_at`: ISO timestamp text\n\nUser records are stored in SQLite `data.db` in the `users` table:\n\n- `id`: integer primary key\n- `email`: required unique text\n- `password_hash`: required text\n- `created_at`: ISO timestamp text\n\n## Validation\n\n- `url` is required for creation and must be a valid URL.\n- `title` is required for creation and must be a non-empty string.\n- Empty titles are rejected with `400`.\n- `folder_id`, when provided, must reference an existing folder or the request returns `400`.\n- `folder_id`, when provided, must reference a folder owned by the authenticated user.\n- Folder `name` is required and must be a non-empty string.\n- `email` is required for registration and login and must be a valid email address.\n- `password` is required for registration and login and must be at least 8 characters.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

`description` is nullable.\n- UI surfaces: none.\n\n## Validation\n\n| Layer | Expected proof |\n| --- | --- |\n| Unit | TypeScript build catches route/database typing issues. |\n| Integration | Curl checks against local server cover create, list, read, update, delete, validation, health, and SQLite persistence. |\n| E2E | Not applicable; API-only task. |\n| Platform | Not applicable beyond local Node/SQLite runtime. |\n| Release | Not applicable. |\n\n## Harness Delta\n\nAdded the story packet and bookmark product contract for the T2 slice.\n\n## Evidence\n\n- `npm run build`\n- Local HTTP checks on `PORT=3100`: health `200`, create `201`, list `200`, get `200`, update `200`, delete `204`, deleted get `404`.\n- Validation checks on `POST /bookmarks`: missing title `400`, missing url `400`, empty title `400`.\n- Direct SQLite check confirmed the `bookmarks` schema and a persisted row in `data.db`.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

create/update bookmarks with optional folder reference.\n- Queries: list folders, fetch folder by id, fetch bookmarks for folder, validate folder existence.\n- API: Express JSON handlers under `/folders` plus updated `/bookmarks` handlers.\n- Tables: SQLite `folders` table; nullable `bookmarks.folder_id` foreign key.\n- Domain rules: folder `name` must be a non-empty string; `folder_id` must be `null`, omitted, or an existing positive integer folder id.\n- UI surfaces: none.\n\n## Validation\n\n| Layer | Expected proof |\n| --- | --- |\n| Unit | TypeScript build catches route/database typing issues. |\n| Integration | Local HTTP checks cover folder CRUD, bookmark folder assignment, invalid folder validation, delete nullification, and bookmark regression. |\n| E2E | Not applicable; benchmark/runs/phase-3-active-observability-cli-1.4.1-rerun-20260531/T6-pagination/events.jsonl:10:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_4","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' README.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark\n\nA controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.\n\n## What This Is\n\nA pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### Prerequisites\n\n```bash\n# Codex CLI (primary agent)\nnpm install -g @openai/codex\nexport OPENAI_API_KEY=\"sk-...\"\n\n# System dependencies\nnode --version  # v20+\njq --version    # for JSON parsing\nsqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks\n```\n\n### Run the Benchmark\n\n```bash\n# Baseline run (against harness main branch)\n./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline\n\n# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)\n./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2\n\n# Compare results\n./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2\n```\n\n### View Results\n\n```bash\ncat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary\ncat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores\n```\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\nharness-benchmark/\n├── README.md                 # This file\n├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)\n├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)\n├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration\n├── src/\n│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)\n├── benchmark/\n│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules\n│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script\n│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results\n│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)\n│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists\n│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts\n│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation\n│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry\n│   │   ├── check-functional.sh\n│   │   ├── check-harness.sh\n│   │   ├── check-quality.sh\n│   │   └── report.sh\n│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)\n│   │   └── .gitkeep\n│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs\n│       └── .gitkeep\n```\n\n## The Benchmark Cycle\n\n1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`\n2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)\n3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI\n4. **Score** against objective rubrics\n5. **Compare** to previous runs\n\nEach phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.\n\n## Tasks Overview\n\n| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |\n|------|------|-----------|---------------|\n| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |\n| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |\n| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |\n| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |\n| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |\n| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |\n\n## Metrics\n\n| Metric | What It Measures | Source |\n|--------|-----------------|--------|\n| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |\n| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |\n| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |\n| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |\n| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |\n| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |\n\n## License\n\nMIT\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
benchmark/runs/phase-3-active-observability-cli-1.4.1-rerun-20260531/T6-pagination/events.jsonl:18:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_8","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API\n\n## Overview\n\nA REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.\n\n## Functional Requirements\n\n### Core Entities\n\n- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps\n- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks\n- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders\n\n### API Endpoints\n\n#### Health\n- `GET /health` → `{ \"status\": \"ok\" }`\n\n#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)\n- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)\n- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)\n- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark\n- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark\n- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark\n\n#### Folders\n- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)\n- `GET /folders` — List all folders\n- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks\n- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name\n- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)\n\n#### Authentication\n- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)\n- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token\n- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented\n\n### Validation Rules\n\n- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL\n- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string\n- Folder `name` must be non-empty string\n- Email must be valid format\n- Password must be >= 8 characters\n\n### Pagination (after T6)\n\n- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`\n- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`\n- Default: page=1, limit=20\n- Max limit: 100\n\n## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)\n\n- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+\n- **Language**: TypeScript\n- **Framework**: Express\n- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3\n- **Testing**: Vitest\n- **Port**: 3000\n\n## Non-Functional Requirements\n\n- All responses are JSON\n- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)\n- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)\n- JWT tokens for session management\n- Database file: `data.db` (application data)\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
benchmark/runs/phase-3-active-observability-cli-1.4.1-rerun-20260531/T6-pagination/events.jsonl:20:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_9","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' docs/FEATURE_INTAKE.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Feature Intake\n\nEvery implementation prompt enters the intake gate before code changes. A new\nproject spec also enters through this gate before it becomes product docs,\nstories, or implementation work.\n\nThe human does not need to classify risk. The harness does.\n\n## Intake Flow\n\n```text\nUser prompt\n    |\n    v\nClassify input type\n    |\n    v\nRestate as work item\n    |\n    v\nFind affected product docs and stories\n    |\n    v\nRun risk checklist\n    |\n    v\nChoose lane: tiny, normal, or high-risk\n```\n\n## Input Types\n\nUse the input type to decide where the work should land before choosing the risk\nlane.\n\n| Type | Use when | Typical artifact |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| New spec | Turning a user-provided project spec into harness-ready docs | Product docs, candidate epics, decisions |\n| Spec slice | Implementing selected behavior from an accepted spec | Story packet |\n| Change request | Changing, fixing, or refining accepted behavior | Story packet or direct patch |\n| New initiative | Adding a larger product area that needs multiple stories | Initiative notes plus story packets |\n| Maintenance request | Changing technical, operational, or dependency behavior | Story packet, validation report, or decision |\n| Harness improvement | Improving how humans and agents collaborate | Direct docs update or `scripts/harness backlog add` |\n\nDo not create or extend a monolithic spec by default after intake. Use product\ndocs, stories, decisions, and initiative notes as the living surface.\n\n## Lanes\n\n### Tiny\n\nUse for low-risk docs, copy, names, or narrow edits.\n\nAlso use for initial project setup when the work is limited to installing\ndeclared dependencies, wiring a server entrypoint, adding a health/smoke\nendpoint, or opening a local development database connection without creating\ndomain schema, CRUD behavior, auth, authorization, provider integration, or\ndata migration. A health endpoint in a new benchmark or scaffolded project is\nsmoke proof, not a public contract escalation by itself.\n\nRequirements:\n\n- Patch directly.\n- Keep affected docs current.\n- Run available quick checks.\n- Update the harness only if friction was found.\n\n### Normal\n\nUse for story-sized behavior with bounded blast radius.\n\nRequirements:\n\n- Create or update one story file from `docs/templates/story.md`.\n- Link relevant product docs.\n- Add or update validation expectations.\n- Implement the smallest vertical slice when implementation exists.\n- Record or update proof status with `scripts/harness story add` and\n  `scripts/harness story update`.\n\n### High-Risk\n\nUse when the work can affect security, data, scope, contracts, or multiple\nroles/platforms.\n\nRequirements:\n\n- Create a story folder using `docs/templates/high-risk-story/`.\n- Fill in `execplan.md`, `overview.md`, `design.md`, and `validation.md`.\n- Ask for human confirmation before implementation if direction is ambiguous.\n- Record a decision when behavior or architecture changes meaningfully.\n\n## Risk Checklist\n\nMark one flag for each item that applies:\n\n| Risk flag | Applies when the work touches |\n| --- | --- |\n| Auth | login, logout, sessions, JWT, password, refresh token |\n| Authorization | roles, permissions, tenant or company scope |\n| Data model | schema, migrations, uniqueness, deletion, retention |\n| Audit/security | audit logs, privacy, sensitive data, access logs |\n| External systems | email, payments, cloud services, provider SDKs, queues, webhooks |\n| Public contracts | API shape, response envelope, client-visible behavior |\n| Cross-platform | desktop/mobile/browser split, native shell behavior, deep links |\n| Existing behavior | already implemented or test-covered behavior changes |\n| Weak proof | unclear or missing tests around the affected area |\n| Multi-domain | more than one product domain changes at once |\n\n## Classification\n\n```text\n0-1 flags:\n  tiny or normal, based on code impact\n\n2-3 flags:\n  normal with stronger validation\n\n4+ flags:\n  high-risk\n\nAny hard gate:\n  high-risk unless the human explicitly narrows scope\n```\n\nHard gates:\n\n- Auth.\n- Authorization.\n- Data loss or migration.\n- Audit/security.\n- External provider behavior.\n- Removing or weakening validation requirements.\n\n## Output\n\nAt the end of intake, the agent should be able to say:\n\n```text\nLane: normal\nReason: touches authorization, API contract, and audit benchmark/runs/phase-3-active-observability-cli-1.4.1-rerun-20260531/T6-pagination/events.jsonl:33:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_16","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' docs/product/bookmarks.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Bookmarks\n\nBookmarks are saved URLs with a title, optional description, optional folder, and creation/update timestamps. Folders are named collections of bookmarks. Users own their bookmarks and folders.\n\n## API Contract\n\n- `POST /auth/register` creates a user from valid `email` and `password`, stores a password hash, and returns the user without `password_hash`.\n- `POST /auth/login` authenticates a user from valid `email` and `password` and returns a JWT token.\n- All `/bookmarks` and `/folders` endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return `401` when the token is missing or invalid.\n- `POST /bookmarks` creates an authenticated user's bookmark from `url`, `title`, optional `description`, and optional `folder_id`.\n- `GET /bookmarks` returns the authenticated user's bookmarks as a JSON array.\n- `GET /bookmarks/:id` returns one owned bookmark or `404` when it does not exist for that user.\n- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` updates an owned bookmark, including optional `folder_id`, and returns it, or `404` when it does not exist for that user.\n- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` removes an owned bookmark and returns `204`, or `404` when it does not exist for that user.\n- `POST /folders` creates an authenticated user's folder from required `name` and returns `201`.\n- `GET /folders` returns the authenticated user's folders as a JSON array.\n- `GET /folders/:id` returns one owned folder with a `bookmarks` array or `404` when it does not exist for that user.\n- `PUT /folders/:id` updates an owned folder name and returns it, or `404` when it does not exist for that user.\n- `DELETE /folders/:id` removes an owned folder and returns `204`, or `404` when it does not exist for that user. Owned bookmarks in the deleted folder are not deleted; their `folder_id` becomes `null`.\n\n## Authentication\n\n- Register and login require a valid email address and a password of at least 8 characters.\n- Duplicate registration returns `409`.\n- Invalid login credentials return `401`.\n- JWT payloads include `userId`.\n- Authenticated bookmark and folder responses do not include password hashes.\n\n## Data Contract\n\nBookmark records are stored in SQLite `data.db` in the `bookmarks` table:\n\n- `id`: integer primary key\n- `user_id`: required integer foreign key to `users.id`\n- `url`: required text\n- `title`: required text\n- `description`: nullable text\n- `folder_id`: nullable integer foreign key to `folders.id`\n- `created_at`: ISO timestamp text\n- `updated_at`: ISO timestamp text\n\nFolder records are stored in SQLite `data.db` in the `folders` table:\n\n- `id`: integer primary key\n- `user_id`: required integer foreign key to `users.id`\n- `name`: required text\n- `created_at`: ISO timestamp text\n- `updated_at`: ISO timestamp text\n\nUser records are stored in SQLite `data.db` in the `users` table:\n\n- `id`: integer primary key\n- `email`: required unique text\n- `password_hash`: required text\n- `created_at`: ISO timestamp text\n\n## Validation\n\n- `url` is required for creation and must be a valid URL.\n- `url`, when provided on update, must be a non-empty valid URL.\n- `title` is required for creation and must be a non-empty string after trimming whitespace.\n- `title`, when provided on update, must be a non-empty string after trimming whitespace.\n- Empty or whitespace-only titles and URLs are rejected with `400`.\n- `folder_id`, when provided, must reference an existing folder or the request returns `400`.\n- `folder_id`, when provided, must reference a folder owned by the authenticated user.\n- Folder `name` is required for creation and must be a non-empty string after trimming whitespace.\n- Folder `name`, when provided on update, must be a non-empty string after trimming whitespace.\n- `email` is required for registration and login and must be a valid email address.\n- `password` is required for registration and login and must be at least 8 characters.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### Prerequisites\n\n```bash\n# Codex CLI (primary agent)\nnpm install -g @openai/codex\nexport OPENAI_API_KEY=\"sk-...\"\n\n# System dependencies\nnode --version  # v20+\njq --version    # for JSON parsing\nsqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks\n```\n\n### Run the Benchmark\n\n```bash\n# Baseline run (against harness main branch)\n./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline\n\n# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)\n./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2\n\n# Compare results\n./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2\n```\n\n### View Results\n\n```bash\ncat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary\ncat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores\n```\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\nharness-benchmark/\n├── README.md                 # This file\n├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)\n├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)\n├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration\n├── src/\n│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)\n├── benchmark/\n│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules\n│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script\n│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results\n│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)\n│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists\n│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts\n│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation\n│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry\n│   │   ├── check-functional.sh\n│   │   ├── check-harness.sh\n│   │   ├── check-quality.sh\n│   │   └── report.sh\n│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)\n│   │   └── .gitkeep\n│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs\n│       └── .gitkeep\n```\n\n## The Benchmark Cycle\n\n1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`\n2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)\n3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI\n4. **Score** against objective rubrics\n5. **Compare** to previous runs\n\nEach phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.\n\n## Tasks Overview\n\n| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |\n|------|------|-----------|---------------|\n| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |\n| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |\n| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |\n| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |\n| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |\n| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |\n\n## Metrics\n\n| Metric | What It Measures | Source |\n|--------|-----------------|--------|\n| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |\n| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |\n| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |\n| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |\n| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |\n| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |\n\n## License\n\nMIT\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
benchmark/runs/phase-4-mechanical-verification-20260601/T1-project-setup/events.jsonl:13:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_5","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' docs/FEATURE_INTAKE.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Feature Intake\n\nEvery implementation prompt enters the intake gate before code changes. A new\nproject spec also enters through this gate before it becomes product docs,\nstories, or implementation work.\n\nThe human does not need to classify risk. The harness does.\n\n## Intake Flow\n\n```text\nUser prompt\n    |\n    v\nClassify input type\n    |\n    v\nRestate as work item\n    |\n    v\nFind affected product docs and stories\n    |\n    v\nRun risk checklist\n    |\n    v\nChoose lane: tiny, normal, or high-risk\n```\n\n## Input Types\n\nUse the input type to decide where the work should land before choosing the risk\nlane.\n\n| Type | Use when | Typical artifact |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| New spec | Turning a user-provided project spec into harness-ready docs | Product docs, candidate epics, decisions |\n| Spec slice | Implementing selected behavior from an accepted spec | Story packet |\n| Change request | Changing, fixing, or refining accepted behavior | Story packet or direct patch |\n| New initiative | Adding a larger product area that needs multiple stories | Initiative notes plus story packets |\n| Maintenance request | Changing technical, operational, or dependency behavior | Story packet, validation report, or decision |\n| Harness improvement | Improving how humans and agents collaborate | Direct docs update or `scripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add` |\n\nDo not create or extend a monolithic spec by default after intake. Use product\ndocs, stories, decisions, and initiative notes as the living surface.\n\n## Lanes\n\n### Tiny\n\nUse for low-risk docs, copy, names, or narrow edits.\n\nAlso use for initial project setup when the work is limited to installing\ndeclared dependencies, wiring a server entrypoint, adding a health/smoke\nendpoint, or opening a local development database connection without creating\ndomain schema, CRUD behavior, auth, authorization, provider integration, or\ndata migration. A health endpoint in a new benchmark or scaffolded project is\nsmoke proof, not a public contract escalation by itself.\n\nRequirements:\n\n- Patch directly.\n- Keep affected docs current.\n- Run available quick checks.\n- Update the harness only if friction was found.\n\n### Normal\n\nUse for story-sized behavior with bounded blast radius.\n\nRequirements:\n\n- Create or update one story file from `docs/templates/story.md`.\n- Link relevant product docs.\n- Add or update validation expectations.\n- Implement the smallest vertical slice when implementation exists.\n- Record or update proof status with `scripts/bin/harness-cli story add` and\n  `scripts/bin/harness-cli story update`.\n\n### High-Risk\n\nUse when the work can affect security, data, scope, contracts, or multiple\nroles/platforms.\n\nRequirements:\n\n- Create a story folder using `docs/templates/high-risk-story/`.\n- Fill in `execplan.md`, `overview.md`, `design.md`, and `validation.md`.\n- Ask for human confirmation before implementation if direction is ambiguous.\n- Record a durable decision when behavior, architecture, authorization, data\n  ownership, API shape, or validation requirements change meaningfully. Use a\n  `docs/decisions/NNNN-*.md` file from `docs/templates/decision.md`, then add\n  or refresh the durable row with `scripts/bin/harness-cli decision add`.\n  Decision text in a trace is not a durable decision record.\n\n## Risk Checklist\n\nMark one flag for each item that applies:\n\n| Risk flag | Applies when the work touches |\n| --- | --- |\n| Auth | login, logout, sessions, JWT, password, refresh token |\n| Authorization | roles, permissions, tenant or company scope |\n| Data model | schema, migrations, uniqueness, deletion, retention |\n| Audit/security | audit logs, privacy, sensitive data, access logs |\n| External systems | email, payments, cloud services, provider SDKs, queues, webhooks |\n| Public contracts | API shape, response envelope, client-visible behavior |\n| Cross-platform | desktop/mobile/browser split, native shell behavior, deep links |\n| Existing behavior | already implemented or test-covered behavior changes |\n| Weak proof | unclear or missing tests around the affected area |\n| Multi-domain | more than one product domain changes at once |\n\n## Classification\n\n```text\n0-1 flags:\n  tiny or normal, based on code impact\n\n2-3 flags:\n  normal with stronger validation\n\n4+ flags:\n  high-risk\n\nAny hard gate:\n  high-risk unless the human explicitly narrows scope\n```\n\nHard gates:\n\n- Auth.\n- Authorization.\n- Data loss or migration.\n- Audit/security.\n- External provider behavior.\n- Removing or weakening validation requirements.\n\n## Output\n\nAt the end of intake, the agent should be able to say:\n\n```text\nLane: normal\nReason: touches authorization, API contract, and audit behavior.\nDocs: permissions, account-settings, audit-log.\nStory: docs/stories/epics/E02-access-control/US-014-manager-updates-role.md.\nValidation: unit, integration, E2E.\n```\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
benchmark/runs/phase-4-mechanical-verification-20260601/T1-project-setup/events.jsonl:20:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_9","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API\n\n## Overview\n\nA REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.\n\n## Functional Requirements\n\n### Core Entities\n\n- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps\n- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks\n- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders\n\n### API Endpoints\n\n#### Health\n- `GET /health` → `{ \"status\": \"ok\" }`\n\n#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)\n- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)\n- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)\n- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark\n- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark\n- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark\n\n#### Folders\n- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)\n- `GET /folders` — List all folders\n- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks\n- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name\n- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)\n\n#### Authentication\n- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)\n- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token\n- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented\n\n### Validation Rules\n\n- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL\n- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string\n- Folder `name` must be non-empty string\n- Email must be valid format\n- Password must be >= 8 characters\n\n### Pagination (after T6)\n\n- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`\n- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`\n- Default: page=1, limit=20\n- Max limit: 100\n\n## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)\n\n- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+\n- **Language**: TypeScript\n- **Framework**: Express\n- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3\n- **Testing**: Vitest\n- **Port**: 3000\n\n## Non-Functional Requirements\n\n- All responses are JSON\n- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)\n- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)\n- JWT tokens for session management\n- Database file: `data.db` (application data)\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-diagnostic-20260525/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:17:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_7","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API\n\n## Overview\n\nA REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.\n\n## Functional Requirements\n\n### Core Entities\n\n- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps\n- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks\n- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders\n\n### API Endpoints\n\n#### Health\n- `GET /health` → `{ \"status\": \"ok\" }`\n\n#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)\n- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)\n- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)\n- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark\n- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark\n- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark\n\n#### Folders\n- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)\n- `GET /folders` — List all folders\n- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks\n- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name\n- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)\n\n#### Authentication\n- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)\n- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token\n- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented\n\n### Validation Rules\n\n- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL\n- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string\n- Folder `name` must be non-empty string\n- Email must be valid format\n- Password must be >= 8 characters\n\n### Pagination (after T6)\n\n- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`\n- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`\n- Default: page=1, limit=20\n- Max limit: 100\n\n## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)\n\n- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+\n- **Language**: TypeScript\n- **Framework**: Express\n- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3\n- **Testing**: Vitest\n- **Port**: 3000\n\n## Non-Functional Requirements\n\n- All responses are JSON\n- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)\n- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)\n- JWT tokens for session management\n- Database file: `data.db` (application data)\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

id : null;\n}\n\nfunction parseFolderId(value: unknown): number | null | undefined {\n  if (value === undefined) {\n    return undefined;\n  }\n\n  if (value === null) {\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  if (typeof value === \"number\") {\n    return Number.isInteger(value) && value > 0 ? value : undefined;\n  }\n\n  if (typeof value === \"string\" && /^\\d+$/.test(value)) {\n    const id = Number(value);\n    return id > 0 ? id : undefined;\n  }\n\n  return undefined;\n}\n\nfunction findBookmark(id: number): Bookmark | undefined {\n  return db\n    .prepare(`SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?`)\n    .get(id) as Bookmark | undefined;\n}\n\nfunction folderExists(id: number): boolean {\n  const row = db.prepare(\"SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ?\").get(id);\n  return row !== undefined;\n}\n\nrouter.post(\"/\", (req, res) => {\n  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;\n\n  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {\n    res.status(400).json({ error: \"url is required and must be valid\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {\n    res.status(400).json({ error: \"title is required\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const now = new Date().toISOString();\n  const description =\n    typeof body.description === \"string\" ? body.description : null;\n  const folderId = parseFolderId(body.folder_id);\n\n  if (folderId === undefined && body.folder_id !== undefined) {\n    res\n      .status(400)\n      .json({ error: \"folder_id must reference an existing folder\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (typeof folderId === \"number\" && !folderExists(folderId)) {\n    res\n      .status(400)\n      .json({ error: \"folder_id must reference an existing folder\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const result = db\n    .prepare(\n      `\n        INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at)\n        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n      `,\n    )\n    .run(\n      body.url.trim(),\n      body.title.trim(),\n      description,\n      folderId ?? null,\n      now,\n      now,\n    );\n\n  const bookmark = findBookmark(Number(result.lastInsertRowid));\n  res.status(201).json(bookmark);\n});\n\nrouter.get(\"/\", (_req, res) => {\n  const bookmarks = db\n    .prepare(`SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks ORDER BY id ASC`)\n    .all() as Bookmark[];\n\n  res.status(200).json(bookmarks);\n});\n\nrouter.get(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n  const id = parseId(req.params.id);\n  const bookmark = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id);\n\n  if (!bookmark) {\n    res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  res.status(200).json(bookmark);\n});\n\nrouter.put(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n  const id = parseId(req.params.id);\n  const existing = id === null ? body.description : null;\n  }\n\n  if (body.folder_id !== undefined) {\n    const parsedFolderId = parseFolderId(body.folder_id);\n\n    if (parsedFolderId === undefined) {\n      res\n        .status(400)\n        .json({ error: \"folder_id must reference an existing folder\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (typeof parsedFolderId === \"number\" && !folderExists(parsedFolderId)) {\n      res\n        .status(400)\n        .json({ error: \"folder_id must reference an existing folder\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    folderId = parsedFolderId;\n  }\n\n  const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();\n\n  db.prepare(\n    `\n      UPDATE bookmarks\n      SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, folder_id = ?, updated_at = ?\n      WHERE id = ?\n    `,\n  ).run(url, title, description, folderId, updatedAt, id);\n\n  res.status(200).json(findBookmark(id));\n});\n\nrouter.delete(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n  const id = parseId(req.params.id);\n  const result =\n    id === null\n      ? { changes: 0 }\n      : db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?\").run(id);\n\n  if (result.changes === 0) {\n    res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  res.status(204).send();\n});\n\nexport default router;\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-diagnostic-20260525/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:30:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_13","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' src/db.ts\"","aggregated_output":"import Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\n\nexport const db = new Database(\"data.db\");\n\ndb.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\ndb.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n\nexport function initializeDatabase(): void {\n  db.exec(`\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS folders (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n      name TEXT NOT NULL,\n      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,\n      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL\n    );\n\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bookmarks (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n      url TEXT NOT NULL,\n      title TEXT NOT NULL,\n      description TEXT,\n      folder_id INTEGER,\n      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,\n      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,\n      FOREIGN KEY (folder_id) REFERENCES folders(id)\n    )\n  `);\n\n  const columns = db.prepare(\"PRAGMA table_info(bookmarks)\").all() as Array<{\n    name: string;\n  }>;\n\n  if (!columns.some((column) => column.name === \"folder_id\")) {\n    db.exec(\n      \"ALTER TABLE bookmarks ADD COLUMN folder_id INTEGER REFERENCES folders(id)\",\n    );\n  }\n}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-diagnostic-20260525/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:43:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_21","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' docs/stories/T3-folder-support.md\"","aggregated_output":"# T3 Folder Support\n\n## Status\n\nimplemented\n\n## Lane\n\nnormal\n\n## Product Contract\n\nThe API exposes CRUD operations for folders. Bookmarks can optionally reference\na folder through nullable `folder_id`; deleting a folder leaves its bookmarks in\nplace and clears their `folder_id`.\n\n## Relevant Product Docs\n\n- `docs/product/api.md`\n- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n\n- `POST /folders` creates a folder with required `name`.\n- `GET /folders` returns an array of folders.\n- `GET /folders/:id` returns a folder with its bookmarks or HTTP `404`.\n- `PUT /folders/:id` updates a folder name or returns HTTP `404`.\n- `DELETE /folders/:id` deletes a folder with HTTP `204`, returns HTTP `404`\n  when missing, and nullifies assigned bookmarks.\n- `POST /bookmarks` and `PUT /bookmarks/:id` accept optional `folder_id`.\n- Non-existent `folder_id` values return HTTP `400`.\n- Existing bookmark CRUD endpoints continue to work.\n\n## Design Notes\n\n- Commands: create, update, and delete folder rows; assign and unassign bookmark\n  `folder_id`.\n- Queries: list folders, get a folder with its bookmarks, and existing bookmark\n  reads include `folder_id`.\n- API: `/folders` collection and `/folders/:id` item routes.\n- Tables: `folders`; `bookmarks.folder_id`.\n- Domain rules: folder `name` is required; bookmark `folder_id` must reference\n  an existing folder when provided.\n- UI surfaces: none.\n\n## Validation\n\n| Layer | Expected proof |\n| --- | --- |\n| Unit | TypeScript build succeeds. |\n| Integration | Curl checks cover folder CRUD, folder bookmark membership, folder deletion nullification, invalid `folder_id`, and bookmark regression. |\n| E2E | Not applicable for API-only story. |\n| Platform | SQLite `data.db` migrates existing `bookmarks` tables with nullable `folder_id`. |\n| Release | Not applicable for benchmark task. |\n\n## Harness Delta\n\nNo harness process changes were needed.\n\n## Evidence\n\n- Implementation changed `src/db.ts`, `src/index.ts`, `src/bookmarks.ts`, and\n  `src/folders.ts`.\n- Product docs changed `docs/product/api.md`.\n- `npm run build` could not run because dependencies are not installed in this\n  sandbox (`tsc: command not found`).\n- `npm install --offline --prefer-offline --no-audit --no-fund` failed because\n  `@types/bcryptjs` was not present in the local npm cache.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
benchmark/runs/baseline-main-diagnostic-20260525/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:49:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_24","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"find docs/product -maxdepth 2 -type f -print -exec sed -n '1,240p' {} \\\\;\"","aggregated_output":"docs/product/api.md\n# Bookmark Manager API\n\n## Runtime\n\n- The API runs as a Node.js 20+ TypeScript service using Express.\n- The development server listens on port `3000` unless `PORT` is provided.\n- Application data is stored in SQLite at `data.db`.\n\n## Health\n\n### `GET /health`\n\nReturns HTTP `200` with:\n\n```json\n{ \"status\": \"ok\" }\n```\n\n## Bookmarks\n\nBookmarks are persisted in the `bookmarks` SQLite table in `data.db`.\nBookmarks may optionally belong to a folder through nullable `folder_id`.\n\n### `POST /bookmarks`\n\nCreates a bookmark. The JSON body must include non-empty `url` and `title`\nstrings. `description` and `folder_id` are optional and may be omitted.\n\nReturns HTTP `201` with the created bookmark.\n\nReturns HTTP `400` when `url` or `title` is missing or empty.\nReturns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an\nexisting folder.\n\n### `GET /bookmarks`\n\nReturns HTTP `200` with an array of all bookmarks ordered by `id`.\n\n### `GET /bookmarks/:id`\n\nReturns HTTP `200` with the bookmark when it exists.\n\nReturns HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for `id`.\n\n### `PUT /bookmarks/:id`\n\nUpdates `url`, `title`, `description`, and/or `folder_id` for an existing\nbookmark and refreshes `updated_at`.\n\nReturns HTTP `200` with the updated bookmark, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark\nexists for `id`.\n\nReturns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an\nexisting folder.\n\n### `DELETE /bookmarks/:id`\n\nDeletes a bookmark.\n\nReturns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for\n`id`.\n\n## Folders\n\nFolders are persisted in the `folders` SQLite table in `data.db`.\n\n### `POST /folders`\n\nCreates a folder. The JSON body must include a non-empty `name` string.\n\nReturns HTTP `201` with the created folder.\n\nReturns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing or empty.\n\n### `GET /folders`\n\nReturns HTTP `200` with an array of all folders ordered by `id`.\n\n### `GET /folders/:id`\n\nReturns HTTP `200` with the folder and a `bookmarks` array containing bookmarks\nassigned to the folder.\n\nReturns HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.\n\n### `PUT /folders/:id`\n\nUpdates the folder `name` and refreshes `updated_at`.\n\nReturns HTTP `200` with the updated folder, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists\nfor `id`.\n\nReturns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing or empty.\n\n### `DELETE /folders/:id`\n\nDeletes a folder. Bookmarks assigned to the deleted folder remain in the\n`bookmarks` table and have `folder_id` set to `null`.\n\nReturns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.\ndocs/product/README.md\n# Product Docs\n\nThis directory is intentionally generic and mostly empty in Harness v0.\n\nWhen a user provides a project spec, derive smaller product contract files here\ninstead of keeping one large spec as the living plan. Name files by the product\ndomains that actually exist in that spec, for example `overview.md`,\n`billing.md`, `workflows.md`, `permissions.md`, or `api-conventions.md`.\n\nDo not create domain files before the spec just to fill the folder. Empty\nstructure is healthier than fake product truth.\n\n## Update Rule\n\nWhen behavior changes:\n\n1. Update the affected product doc.\n2. Update or create the story packet.\n3. Update durable proof status with `scripts/harness story add` or\n   `scripts/harness story update`.\n4. Record a decision if the change affects architecture, scope, risk, or a\n   previously settled product rule.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

ORDER BY id ASC`,\n    )\n    .all(id, userId) as Bookmark[];\n\n  res.status(200).json({ ...folder, bookmarks });\n});\n\nrouter.put(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n  const userId = req.userId as number;\n  const id = parseId(req.params.id);\n  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id, userId);\n\n  if (!existing || id === null) {\n    res.status(404).json({ error: \"folder not found\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const body = req.body as FolderBody;\n\n  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {\n    res.status(400).json({ error: \"name must be non-empty\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  db.prepare(\n    `\n      UPDATE folders\n      SET name = ?, updated_at = ?\n      WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\n    `,\n  ).run(body.name.trim(), new Date().toISOString(), id, userId);\n\n  res.status(200).json(findFolder(id, userId));\n});\n\nrouter.delete(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n  const userId = req.userId as number;\n  const id = parseId(req.params.id);\n  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id, userId);\n\n  if (!existing || id === null) {\n    res.status(404).json({ error: \"folder not found\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const deleteFolder = db.transaction((folderId: number, ownerId: number) => {\n    db.prepare(\n      \"UPDATE bookmarks SET folder_id = NULL, updated_at = ? WHERE folder_id = ? AND user_id = ?\",\n    ).run(new Date().toISOString(), folderId, ownerId);\n    db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\").run(\n      folderId,\n      ownerId,\n    );\n  });\n\n  deleteFolder(id, userId);\n  res.status(204).send();\n});\n\nexport default router;\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

AND user_id = ?`)\n    .get(id, userId) as Bookmark | undefined;\n}\n\nfunction folderExists(id: number, userId: number): boolean {\n  const row = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n    .get(id, userId);\n  return row !== undefined;\n}\n\nrouter.post(\"/\", (req, res) => {\n  const userId = req.userId as number;\n  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;\n\n  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {\n    res.status(400).json({ error: \"url is required and must be valid\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {\n    res.status(400).json({ error: \"title is required\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const now = new Date().toISOString();\n  const description =\n    typeof body.description === \"string\" ? body.description : null;\n  const folderId = parseFolderId(body.folder_id);\n\n  if (folderId === undefined && body.folder_id !== undefined) {\n    res\n      .status(400)\n      .json({ error: \"folder_id must reference an existing folder\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (typeof folderId === \"number\" && !folderExists(folderId, userId)) {\n    res\n      .status(400)\n      .json({ error: \"folder_id must reference an existing folder\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const result = db\n    .prepare(\n      `\n        INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, description, folder_id, user_id, created_at, updated_at)\n        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n      `,\n    )\n    .run(\n      body.url.trim(),\n      body.title.trim(),\n      description,\n      folderId ?? null,\n      userId,\n      now,\n      now,\n    );\n\n  const bookmark = findBookmark(Number(result.lastInsertRowid), userId);\n  res.status(201).json(bookmark);\n});\n\nrouter.get(\"/\", (req, res) => {\n  const userId = req.userId as number;\n  const bookmarks = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,\n    )\n    .all(userId) as Bookmark[];\n\n  res.status(200).json(bookmarks);\n});\n\nrouter.get(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n  const userId = req.userId as number;\n  const id = parseId(req.params.id);\n  const bookmark = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id, userId);\n\n  if (!bookmark) {\n    res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  res.status(200).json(bookmark);\n});\n\nrouter.put(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n  const userId = req.userId as number;\n  const id = parseId(req.params.id);\n  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id, userId);\n\n  if (!existing || id === null) {\n    res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;\n  let url = existing.url;\n  let title = existing.title;\n  let description = existing.description;\n  let folderId = existing.folder_id;\n\n  if (body.url !== undefined) {\n    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {\n      res.status(400).json({ error: \"url must be valid\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    url = body.url.trim();\n  }\n\n  if (body.title !== undefined) {\n    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {\n      res.status(400).json({ error: \"title must be non-empty\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    title = body.title.trim();\n  }\n\n  if (body.description !== undefined) {\n    description = typeof body.description === \"string\" ? body.description : null;\n  }\n\n  if (body.folder_id !== undefined) {\n    const parsedFolderId = parseFolderId(body.folder_id);\n\n    if (parsedFolderId === undefined) {\n      res\n        .status(400)\n        .json({ error: \"folder_id must reference an existing folder\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (\n      typeof parsedFolderId === \"number\" &&\n      !folderExists(parsedFolderId, userId)\n    ) {\n      res\n        .status(400)\n        .json({ error: \"folder_id must reference an existing folder\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    folderId = parsedFolderId;\n  }\n\n  const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();\n\n  db.prepare(\n    `\n      UPDATE bookmarks\n      SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, folder_id = ?, updated_at = ?\n      WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\n    `,\n  ).run(url, title, description, folderId, updatedAt, id, userId);\n\n  res.status(200).json(findBookmark(id, userId));\n});\n\nrouter.delete(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n  const userId = req.userId as number;\n  const id = parseId(req.params.id);\n  const result =\n    id === null\n      ? { changes: 0 }\n      : db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\").run(id, userId);\n\n  if (result.changes === 0) {\n    res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  res.status(204).send();\n});\n\nexport default router;\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

benchmark/runs/baseline-main-diagnostic-20260525/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:133:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_68","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' docs/product/api.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Bookmark Manager API\n\n## Runtime\n\n- The API runs as a Node.js 20+ TypeScript service using Express.\n- The development server listens on port `3000` unless `PORT` is provided.\n- Application data is stored in SQLite at `data.db`.\n\n## Health\n\n### `GET /health`\n\nReturns HTTP `200` with:\n\n```json\n{ \"status\": \"ok\" }\n```\n\n## Authentication\n\nUsers are persisted in the `users` SQLite table in `data.db`. Passwords are\nstored as bcrypt hashes. Bookmark and folder endpoints require a JWT bearer\ntoken after authentication.\n\n### `POST /auth/register`\n\nCreates a user account. The JSON body must include a valid `email` and a\n`password` with at least 8 characters.\n\nReturns HTTP `201` with the created user without `password_hash`.\n\nReturns HTTP `400` when email or password validation fails.\nReturns HTTP `409` when the email is already registered.\n\n### `POST /auth/login`\n\nAuthenticates a user account. The JSON body must include a valid `email` and a\n`password` with at least 8 characters.\n\nReturns HTTP `200` with:\n\n```json\n{ \"token\": \"<jwt>\" }\n```\n\nReturns HTTP `400` when email or password validation fails.\nReturns HTTP `401` when credentials are wrong.\n\n## Bookmarks\n\nBookmarks are persisted in the `bookmarks` SQLite table in `data.db`.\nBookmarks are owned by the authenticated user and may optionally belong to one\nof that user's folders through nullable `folder_id`.\n\nAll bookmark endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return HTTP\n`401` when the token is missing or invalid.\n\n### `POST /bookmarks`\n\nCreates a bookmark. The JSON body must include non-empty `url` and `title`\nstrings. `description` and `folder_id` are optional and may be omitted.\n\nReturns HTTP `201` with the created bookmark.\n\nReturns HTTP `400` when `url` or `title` is missing or empty.\nReturns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an\nexisting folder owned by the authenticated user.\n\n### `GET /bookmarks`\n\nReturns HTTP `200` with an array of the authenticated user's bookmarks ordered\nby `id`.\n\n### `GET /bookmarks/:id`\n\nReturns HTTP `200` with the bookmark when it exists.\n\nReturns HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for `id`.\n\n### `PUT /bookmarks/:id`\n\nUpdates `url`, `title`, `description`, and/or `folder_id` for an existing\nbookmark and refreshes `updated_at`.\n\nReturns HTTP `200` with the updated bookmark, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark\nexists for `id`.\n\nReturns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an\nexisting folder owned by the authenticated user.\n\n### `DELETE /bookmarks/:id`\n\nDeletes a bookmark.\n\nReturns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for\n`id`.\n\n## Folders\n\nFolders are persisted in the `folders` SQLite table in `data.db` and are owned\nby the authenticated user.\n\nAll folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return HTTP\n`401` when the token is missing or invalid.\n\n### `POST /folders`\n\nCreates a folder. The JSON body must include a non-empty `name` string.\n\nReturns HTTP `201` with the created folder.\n\nReturns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing or empty.\n\n### `GET /folders`\n\nReturns HTTP `200` with an array of the authenticated user's folders ordered by\n`id`.\n\n### `GET /folders/:id`\n\nReturns HTTP `200` with the folder and a `bookmarks` array containing the\nauthenticated user's bookmarks assigned to the folder.\n\nReturns HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.\n\n### `PUT /folders/:id`\n\nUpdates the folder `name` and refreshes `updated_at`.\n\nReturns HTTP `200` with the updated folder, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists\nfor `id`.\n\nReturns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing or empty.\n\n### `DELETE /folders/:id`\n\nDeletes a folder. Bookmarks assigned to the deleted folder remain in the\n`bookmarks` table and have `folder_id` set to `null`.\n\nReturns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
